description_long: This sculpture depicts a seated human figure with arms crossed atop its knees, rendered in a stylized, block-like form reminiscent of ancient Egyptian statuary. The figure's facial features are simplified, with a serene expression and prominent ears, and the head is slightly forward-leaning. The body is compact, with the knees drawn up and feet flat on a rectangular base, which is adorned with subtle carved details. The back of the sculpture features a vertical support extending from the base to the head, typical of traditional stone statuary for stability. The entire piece is a uniform off-white color, suggesting it is made from stone or a similar material. The sculpture rests on a thin, darker plinth, possibly wood, which serves as a display base.
description: A stylized, off-white stone sculpture of a seated human figure with arms crossed over its knees, resting on a rectangular base with a vertical back support.
description_view_0: Front view shows the figure's face, crossed arms, and knees drawn up, with feet visible on the base.
description_view_1: Left side view highlights the profile of the head, arms, and legs, showing the compact seated posture and the thickness of the vertical back support.
description_view_2: Rear view reveals the full length of the vertical back support and the back of the head, with the figure's arms and legs mostly obscured.
description_view_3: Right side view mirrors the left, displaying the side profile of the figure and the structure of the back support.
attribute_location_context: The sculpture is displayed on a small plinth, suggesting an indoor or curated outdoor setting such as a museum, gallery, or public plaza.
